calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Apr-05-2012 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Monica Dell'osso for Virgil Salzman (Administrator).

The need for waivers/gifts to the $12,500.00 gift each to the two nephews were addressed. Counsel argued that both parties have signed off on the settlement agreement for the distributions. The matter was taken under submission.

Judge: John Dearman. Clerk: Ella Yip. Bailiff: Deputy C. Garcia.

Petition For Removal Of Administrator Of Estate; For Suspension Of Powers
Off Calendar Nov-15-2011 Continued To Dec-14-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Monica Dell'Osso for Virgil Salzman (Administrator). Mark Senick for Virgil Salzman (as Individual). Henry Glasser for Mark Haines.

Discussions regarding how to reach resolution were held. The Parties agreed to attend a settlement conference with Judge Wiss which is scheduled for December 12, 2011 at 10:30 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Petition for Removal of Administrator and the Accounting Petition were continued to December 14, 2011 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for trial setting.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera/Ella Yip. Bailiff: Deputy A. Whitehead.

MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Henry Glasser for Mark Haines. Mark Seneck for Virgil Salzman (Individual). Monica Dell'Osso for Virgil Salzman (Administrator).

The matter was continued to October 12, 2011 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of sending the matter out to Dept. 206 for trial.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Chien.

Off Calendar Mar-16-2011 Continued To Sep-13-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Henry Glasser for Mark Haines (Beneficiary). Beverly Smith for Virgil Salzman.

Ms. Smith stated that she will file the first account and report within 30-days and that she cannot file a final accounting because there are two properties that need to be sold. A status date for filing of the accounting was set for April 19, 2011 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Further report on status of administration was continued to September 13, 2011 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 (order approving second report on status of administration will be signed).

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

Petition To Determine Ownership Of Estate Property And For Order Authorizing And Directing Transfer Of Estate Property
Off Calendar Jul-26-2010 Continued To Sep-27-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Monica Dell'Osso and Mark Senick for Virgil Salzman. Henry Glasser for Mark Haines.

Counsel agreed to try to have a global settlement. The Court signed the order referring the matter to Pro Bono Mediation. Mediation must be completed no later than September 17, 2010. Counsel must report status of mediation to the Probate Examiner by September 22, 2010. A status conference is set for September 27, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Santiago.

Off Calendar Apr-05-2010 Continued To Apr-19-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Henry Glasser for Mark Haines. Mark Senick for Virgil Salzman as Petitioner. Monica Dell'Osso for Virgil Salzman as Administrator.

Mr. Senick stated that he had not filed the Substitution of Attorney, that it had been prepared and signed by Ms. Dell'Osso, and that it had been sent to Mr. Salzman. The matter was continued to April 19, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Mr. Glasser and Ms. Dell'Osso will work out the issue with regard to the accounting.

Petition To Determine Ownership Of Estate Property And For Order Authorizing And Directing Transfer Of Estate Property
Off Calendar Feb-16-2010 Continued To Mar-08-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Henry Glasser for Mark Haines. Monica Dell'Osso for Virgil Salzman.

Mr. Glasser waived notice on behalf of his Client. Ms. Dell'Osso stated that the Inventory had not been filed due to having to bring the 850 petition, that there was an error when recording title on the property as the escrow instructions indicated joint tenants and that someone from the Escrow Company verified that the vesting instrucitons had not been followed properly. Mr. Glasser argued that the Parties had time to correct title and that money had been borrowed twice on the property as tenants in common. The Court raised the issue regarding conflict of interest with Mr. Salzman pursuing an individual claim against an Estate for which he is the Administrator. Ms. Dell'Osso stated that she anticipated Mr. Salzman obtaining seperate Counsel for the 850 petition. The matter was continued to March 8, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for filing of a Substitution of Attorney for the Petition to Determine Ownership of Estate Property (850 petition).

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Helen Sorep-Matley, CSR 225. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Capozzoli.
